plan_section: Full CoreScope map sound-mode implementation, Steps 1-6

plan_alignment.notes: The cumulative implementation matches the Forge plan: overlay-owned sound assets, Colorado Mesh branding, controller state, packet routing, procedural modes, bundled orchestral samples, Docker smoke coverage, and documentation are all present without direct vendor/CoreScope edits.

correctness_safety.notes: Audio remains opt-in and user-gesture gated, persisted state is browser-local localStorage only, CoreScope's old audio path is suppressed, normalized sound events avoid message text/sender fields, and no secrets or new server/env requirements were added.

code_quality.notes: The overlay code is defensive around browser APIs, localStorage, Web Audio, sample fetch/decode failures, and DOM timing. The sound router, mode strategies, and sample-loading paths are cohesive and bounded.

completeness.notes: Step acceptance criteria are satisfied, including default Off behavior, saved non-off locked state, volume persistence, old audio suppression, lazy orchestral asset loading, procedural Space Blaster, Docker/static route checks, and README operator notes. Reported lint, typecheck, unit, e2e, Docker build, Docker smoke, and diff checks are adequate.

patterns.notes: The implementation follows the approved overlay architecture, keeps Docker/same-container routing intact, uses existing CoreScope seams instead of vendor edits, and keeps visible user-facing labels aligned to Colorado Mesh.

integration.notes: Overlay injection, nginx routing, shell controls, CoreScope MeshAudio wrapping, orchestral static assets, e2e harness coverage, and Docker smoke coverage integrate cleanly. vendor/CoreScope shows no cumulative diff or dirty working tree.

flags.note: Minor existing flag: ISO/string timestamps passed via received_at are parsed numerically rather than with Date.parse, which can reduce fidelity for synthetic/hashless events.
Root favicon.ico contains PNG image data. Browser metadata points at the correct brand ICO path, so this is non-blocking unless strict root favicon.ico consumers matter.
